text: Last class we ran the vendor call and assigned every action item.
Now the agenda is settled, so today we make our case and negotiate the terms.

Thanks for your voicemail, Klaus. What we are looking for is a fair unit price, and it is the rising cost that worries us most.
I understand. It is the new tariffs that have pushed our costs up. A rebate on past orders is not possible, but a volume discount is.
Then let us find a trade-off. What matters to us is predictability, so a two-year term works if the value proposition improves.
Agreed. It is the longer commitment that lets us offer priority support and faster fulfillment. That is a real win-win.
Good. But a unit price above our budget is our walk-away point. What I need is a firm number today, not an ultimatum next week.
Fair enough. What I can offer is a five percent volume discount and an early-payment incentive. It is the partnership that we value most.
It is the price that concerns us most.
What we need is a firm commitment today.
We can make a trade-off on the term for a better price.
A price above our budget is our walk-away point.
Let us find common ground and close a win-win.

Hi Fabiana, this is Klaus. Before the negotiation tomorrow, I want to be transparent about where TechFlow stands. What we are looking for is a longer contract, and it is the commitment that matters most to us. We can offer a volume discount, but a rebate on past orders is not on the table. It is the rising tariffs that have pushed our costs up, so there is little room on the unit price. What I would suggest is a trade-off: if you accept a two-year term, we can improve the value proposition with faster fulfillment and priority support. Our bottom line is firm, but we want a win-win. If we reach a deadlock on price, let us find common ground on the terms instead. Talk tomorrow.

Hi team, here is a quick recap of the negotiation with TechFlow. It went better than expected. What we secured is a five percent volume discount and faster fulfillment, and it is the longer term that unlocked it. They would not move on a rebate, so we made a trade-off: we accepted a two-year contract, and in return they strengthened their value proposition with priority support. It was the tariff issue that kept the unit price firm, but we held our bottom line on payment terms. At one point we hit a deadlock, and what saved the deal was finding common ground on delivery dates. Overall it is a win-win, and the incentive of a long partnership convinced them. I will send the summary shortly. Let me know if anything needs to be added.

Klaus leaves a voicemail explaining where TechFlow stands. Fabiana opens the negotiation and presents her value proposition. They reach a deadlock on the unit price because of new tariffs. A trade-off is agreed: a longer term in exchange for a volume discount. Both sides close a win-win deal, and the incentive of a long partnership seals it.
